current.summary: Design provenance and attestation model for syllago content types — authorship, versioning, source binding, derivation lineage, content hashing. Applies across skills, agents, hooks, rules, MCP, commands, loadouts.

activeWork.description: Provenance and attestation specification for syllago content — how to express who made it, where it came from, what version it is, whether it's been tampered with, and what it was derived from. Must work across all 7 content types.
activeWork.progress: Extensive prior research and adversarial review completed. Prior panel recommended 8-field minimum sidecar convention. Session pivoted to install safety strategy (separate handoff). Now starting fresh on provenance/attestation with full context from prior work.
blockers: Need to determine: does provenance metadata go in SKILL.md frontmatter, a sidecar file, a collection manifest, or some combination?
Need to determine: what's the minimum viable provenance that works across ALL content types, not just skills?
Content hash algorithm details still open (normalization, blanking regex, binary handling, symlinks)

git.lastCommit: 3b34379 handoff: CDR execution complete, content-based classification gap identified

recentDecisions: Content hash IS part of provenance (not just install safety) — it's the integrity verification piece of the provenance story
No social trust/vouch layer — provenance is factual (who, when, where, what hash), not social (who endorses it)
Facts-only design principle carries over from install safety: provenance records facts, humans judge
Prior adversarial panel consensus: 8-field minimum across 2 sections (provenance + triggers). Triggers now handled separately via SKILL.md frontmatter fields. Provenance fields to revisit fresh.
Sidecar pattern (SKILL.meta.yaml) was architecturally endorsed by prior panel (6/6) but questioned whether it needs multi-tool adoption to be a 'convention' vs just a 'syllago feature'
The spec governance is non-functional (0/48 proposals merged) — any provenance work must route around the spec, not depend on spec changes
nextSteps: Map content types to provenance needs: what provenance fields make sense for skills vs hooks vs MCP configs vs loadouts?
Decide WHERE provenance lives: per-content sidecar, collection manifest, embedded in existing metadata (.syllago.yaml), or computed at publish time only

Design the content hash algorithm (carry forward prior panel recommendations: drop CRLF normalization, mandate LF, specify exact blanking regex, define symlink behavior, reference implementation + 20 test vectors)
Determine minimum viable provenance fields: version, authors, source_repo, content_hash — what else? What about derived_from for converted/forked content?

Consider how provenance interacts with syllago's hub-and-spoke conversion model: when a skill is converted from Claude Code format to Cursor format, the hash changes. How is lineage tracked across conversions?
Run adversarial review on the provenance design before committing to implementation

context.notes:
IMPORTANT BACKGROUND — read these files for full context before designing:

1. docs/research/agent-skills-spec/18-install-safety-strategy.md — the definitive install safety strategy. Content hash is part of this AND part of provenance. Don't duplicate work.

2. docs/spec/skills/archive/15-provenance-deep-dive.md — 6-persona adversarial review of a prior provenance design. Key recommendations: drop CRLF normalization, add published_at timestamp, separate identity from integrity (registry-assigned ID vs content hash), add 'adapt' to derived_from vocabulary, define denouncement record format. Prior panel's concrete 8-field minimum: metadata_spec, version, source_repo, content_hash, authors, mode, activation_file_globs, commands. The last 3 (mode, globs, commands) are now handled by activation/triggers work, not provenance.

3. docs/spec/skills/archive/14-adversarial-review-panel.md — 6-persona review of the full metadata convention. Key kills: durability field (1/12 agents support it), tags (become SEO spam), unsigned attestation shouldn't be called 'attestation'. Key keeps: mode field (unanimous highest value), content hash algorithm (well-engineered), sidecar pattern (architecturally sound).

4. docs/research/agent-skills-spec/07-synthesis.md — community consensus on what must change. Tier 1: path standardization, activation reliability, security primitives, governance. Tier 2: distribution standard, permissions model, remote discovery.

5. The install safety strategy (file 18) already owns: quarantine fetch, behavioral scanning, health signals, dependency auto-install. Provenance owns: authorship, versioning, source binding, content hash spec, derivation lineage, and eventually signing.

SEPARATE HANDOFF exists for install safety implementation: install-safety-strategy-ready-for-implementation.json

Syllago content types (all 7 need provenance): skills, agents, hooks, rules, MCP configs, commands, loadouts.
